content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 12, 1998

Harvey Tsuboi
Office of the
Secretary of State
1500 llth Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Consolidated Campaign Statement
Our File No. I-98-144

Dear Mr. Tsuboi:

This is in response to your question posed to Dixie Howard of this office regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act¹ This letter constitutes written advice under Section 84205.

Section 84205 provides:

The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining statements and reports required to be filed by this title.

A special election has been called for September 1, 1998, to fill the vacancy of Senator Barbara Lee in the 9ᵗʰ Senate District. The first pre-election campaign statement required to be filed for this seat is due July 23, 1998, and covers the period through July 18, 1998. The semi-annual statement filing deadline for candidates and committees qualified prior to July 1, 1998, is July 31, 1998. We believe it appropriate for committees formed prior to July 1 that are candidate-controlled committees running for that office, or committees formed primarily to

¹Government Code Section 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. I-98-144
Page 2

support or oppose candidates running for that office, to file a combined semi-annual and first pre-election statement no later than July 23, 1998, covering a period from January 1, 1998 through July 18, 1998.

If you have any questions regarding this, please feel free to call me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Carla Wardlow, Chief
Technical Assistance Division

By: Jeanette E. Turvin
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

SGC/JET/jt

sections.question_synthetic: May candidate-controlled committees and committees formed to support or oppose candidates in a special election for California State Senate combine their semi-annual and first pre-election campaign statements into a single filing?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that committees formed prior to July 1, 1998, that are candidate-controlled or formed to support/oppose candidates in the September 1, 1998 special election for the 9th Senate District may file a combined semi-annual and first pre-election statement by July 23, 1998, covering January 1 through July 18, 1998.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicit 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers but contains clear question and answer content. Written advice authorized under Government Code Section 84205. Minor OCR artifact ('CorvirvnssloN' likely 'Commission') does not affect extraction quality.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May candidate-controlled committees and committees formed to support or oppose candidates in a special election for California State Senate combine their semi-annual and first pre-election campaign statements into a single filing?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advises that committees formed prior to July 1, 1998, that are candidate-controlled or formed to support/oppose candidates in the September 1, 1998 special election for the 9th Senate District may file a combined semi-annual and first pre-election statement by July 23, 1998, covering January 1 through July 18, 1998.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter permitting the consolidation of semi-annual and pre-election campaign statements for committees participating in the special election to fill State Senator Barbara Lee's 9th District seat in September 1998.
